meta.reportLabel: Wed Report — dollar firm into WARSH's Sintra debut (today, 13:00 GMT) + US NFP (Thu, +130k exp vs +172k). Big story: the YEN broke 162 to a fresh 40-YEAR LOW (~162.3) — the long-JPY core is being run over; MoF now 'when not if' but doubted. Eurozone June flash HICP eased to 3.1% (from 3.2%) — re-softens EUR, re-firms the EUR/USD short. Brent ~$73 after weekend Iran-US Hormuz strikes then a 'stand down' — CAD still weakest.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-firm but two catalysts loom. May core PCE confirmed hot (3.4% / headline ~4.1%) and the June dot flipped the next move to a HIKE, keeping DXY ~101.3 with Sept-hike odds still sub-50%. Warsh makes his first international appearance today (Sintra panel, expected quiet) and June NFP prints Thu (+130k consensus vs May's hot +172k). The overnight story is the yen: USD/JPY broke clean through 162 to a fresh 40-year low (~162.3) on the widest US-Japan rate gap of the cycle + fiscal-dovish Japan — the structural long-JPY core is being squeezed, MoF intervention now 'when not if' but doubted post-April. Eurozone June flash HICP eased to 3.1% (from 3.2%), re-softening the EUR. The cleaner edge stays the oil leg: Brent ~$73 pins CAD near a 14-mo low (USD/CAD ~1.415).
meta.nextBigEvent: Warsh's first Sintra appearance (Wed Jul 1, 13:00 GMT), then US June jobs / NFP (Thu Jul 2, +130k exp) — the dollar's next tests

dailyRead: The dollar carries a firm bid into Warsh's Sintra debut and tomorrow's jobs, and the big overnight story is the yen: USD/JPY broke clean through 162 to a fresh 40-year low (~162.3), running the structural long-JPY core straight over. The break — driven by the widest US-Japan rate gap in the cycle plus a fiscally-dovish Japan — puts spot inside the intervention zone with the MoF now a 'when, not if' (yet effectiveness doubted after April's massive op did little). Warsh makes his first international appearance today (13:00 GMT panel with Lagarde, Bailey, Macklem) — expected to say little, which is exactly why any hawkish tilt moves markets; then June NFP (Thu) prints against a soft +130k consensus vs May's hot +172k, the cleanest fade if it cools. Eurozone June flash HICP eased to 3.1% (from 3.2%), softening the EUR again and re-firming the EUR/USD short after Monday's Sintra-trim. The oil leg stays the cleanest edge: Brent ticked up to ~$73 on weekend Iran-US strikes near Hormuz, but both sides 'stood down' (vessels move freely) so the disinflationary base case holds — CAD stays weakest. Best-expressed: USD/CAD long, AUD/CAD long, EUR/USD short; the long-JPY core (USD/JPY short, JPYBASKET long) is being squeezed to fresh 40-yr lows — reduce/defend, do NOT add; wait for intervention as the re-load.

strength.drivers: Core PCE 3.4% (May, up); headline ~4.1%; CPI 4.2%; NFP +172k (May), unemp 4.3%; Jun consensus +130k. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE (median 3.8%). DXY ~101.3.
strength.forward: 🦅 Warsh speaks today; jobs tomorrow: hot PCE + a hike-dot back a hawkish Warsh, but the in-line print keeps Sept odds sub-50%. Risk: a cool Jul 2 jobs (+130k exp) is the clean fade. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.drivers: Core ~2.5% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); CPI 2.8% (held). BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (Greene + Pill hike votes); next BoE Jul 30 + MPR.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish — services re-accelerated to 3.7%, reopening a hike-tail and keeping 2 dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR. Risk: a soft activity wave re-splits it. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: sticky core, more hikes priced, capped by USD
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%, 60bp above target top); headline ~4.0%; unemp 4.5%. RBA 4.35% (hiked May, 8-1); market prices the cash rate to 4.70% by year-end.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky 3.6% trim + market pricing 4.70% by Dec keeps an Aug-hike tail open; AUD/USD ~0.692, capped sub-0.70 by the dollar. Risk: a cool Q2 jobs / risk-off. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.tag: Jul 9 coin-flip, poll now leans hold
strength.drivers: Headline ~2.5%; RBNZ saw a 4.3% Q3 peak (pre oil-drop); unemp ~5.1% elevated. RBNZ 2.25% (held May 27, casting-vote split). Reuters poll + Westpac now lean hold; UBS/ING still hike; swaps ~18-21bp for Jul 9.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Split into Jul 9, poll now leans hold: Reuters poll + Westpac see a hold at 2.25% (oil slide gutted the 4.3% Q3-peak cost-shock case); UBS/ING still expect a hike (de-anchoring risk over the long Jul-Sept gap). Live two-way — the kiwi's biggest near event. Next RBNZ Jul 9.

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; CPI ~2.8%; Tokyo core re-accelerating. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16), Ueda/Tamura back more hikes; but USD/JPY broke 162 to ~162.3 (lowest yen since 1986) on the rate gap + fiscal-dovish Japan.
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination up, spot at a 40-yr low. Tokyo core re-accelerating backs more BoJ hikes, but the rate gap + fiscal-dovish Japan dominate; intervention now 'when not if' but doubted post-April — two-sided, the swing. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.drivers: HICP 3.1% (Jun flash, down from 3.2%); core ~2.2% (May); unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 17), 'gradual' frame; sees HICP 3.0% '26 -> 2.0% '28.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 June flash eased to 3.1% — re-softens EUR. Cooler print undercuts Monday's firmer-Sintra bounce; Lagarde still 'can raise without fear' but the data leans dovish-of-hawkish. ECB Jul 23 hold 94% priced. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (up from 2.8%, gasoline +33% y/y); trimmed core 2.0%, median 2.1%; unemp ~6.9%, weak retail; technical recession. BoC 2.25% (4th hold, Jun 10), looking through energy.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Hot/broadening CPI gives CAD a floor, but Brent ~$73 keeps the loonie near a 14-mo low (USD/CAD ~1.415); BoC looks through energy. Risk: oil re-spike rescues it. Next BoC Jul 15.

symbols.why: Cleanest pair: firm US dollar into Warsh (hot PCE + hike-dot) vs CAD near a 14-mo low on Brent ~$73. Hot CPI 3.2% only a floor (core ~2%); technical recession. ~1.415.
symbols.risk: An oil re-spike (Hormuz flare / Lebanon) rescues CAD; a cool Jul 2 NFP fades the USD leg.

symbols.why: Cleanest commodity split: sticky AUD trim 3.6% + a live RBA tail (4.70% priced by Dec) vs oil-pressed, look-through-CPI CAD; Brent sub-$73 widens it toward parity.
symbols.risk: Risk-off hits AUD beta harder; a CAD-supportive oil bid.

symbols.why: Re-firmed: June flash HICP eased to 3.1% (softens EUR again), undoing part of the Sintra-trim, into a firm dollar bid ahead of Warsh + jobs.
symbols.risk: A soft Jul 2 NFP lifts it; a dovish-surprise Warsh removes the USD prop.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination + a softer EUR (3.1% flash) — but CHALLENGED: the yen's break to a 40-yr low is running every XXX/JPY higher. Reduce/defend.
symbols.risk: Yen-weakness momentum overwhelms the short until intervention hits; a hawkish EUR surprise.

symbols.why: CAD pinned by Brent sub-$73 tilts it long; the NZD leg is two-sided (Jul 9 poll leans hold), so it is carried by the OIL leg, not rates.
symbols.risk: A dovish RBNZ surprise undercuts NZD; an oil bid lifts CAD.

symbols.why: Structural core (BoJ hiking, coord watch) but RUN OVER: broke 162 to ~162.3, a 40-yr low. Reduce/defend, do NOT add; wait for intervention as the re-load.
symbols.risk: Momentum toward 163-164; a JOINT intervention spike is the only violent snap-back.

symbols.why: Structural-JPY destination vs a CAD pinned by sub-$73 oil — but the yen 40-yr-low break challenges it near-term. Low conviction.
symbols.risk: Yen weakness runs it up; an intervention spike snaps it lower (the re-load).

symbols.why: USD headwind keeps it sub-0.70, but conviction trimmed as the 3.6% trim + 4.70%-priced RBA arm a floor — two-sided into Jul 2.
symbols.risk: A cool Jul 2 NFP + sticky core flips the leg — the firm-AUD invalidation.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: services CPI 3.7% re-armed a hawkish BoE vs a firm-but-fading dollar — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: Soft Jul 2 jobs lifts it; soft UK activity re-splits it.

symbols.why: Jul 9 RBNZ a live two-way (poll leans hold, swaps ~18-21bp), so the soft-NZD lean stays faded — a level-trade vs a firm dollar.
symbols.risk: A hawkish RBNZ repricing or hot NZ data lifts it; a firm Jul 2 NFP presses it.

symbols.why: Services-CPI re-acceleration firmed the GBP leg; the yen 40-yr-low break tilts it higher near-term — two-sided.
symbols.risk: Firm GBP + weak yen spot tilts it up; a coordinated intervention spike snaps it lower.

symbols.why: Weak-yen momentum leans it up near-term, capping the short; only risk-off carry unwind + RBA floor argue lower.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry + yen weakness holds it up; a coordinated spike is the abrupt re-load.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination vs a two-sided NZD — carry + yen weakness offset near-term, low conviction.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry dominates near-term -> stalls; an intervention spike re-loads.

symbols.why: ECB gradual + softer flash (3.1%) vs a hawkish-hold RBA (sticky trim, 4.70% priced) — mild AUD tilt, no USD vector.
symbols.risk: EZ vs Aussie data set direction; a further Sintra/EZ shift.

symbols.why: ECB spent (softer flash) vs a two-sided NZD (Jul 9 coin-flip) — net neutral, mild NZD tilt.
symbols.risk: NZD risk beta or a Jul 23 ECB shift.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: re-armed BoE (services 3.7%) vs hawkish-hold RBA (3.6% trim) — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: UK services vs Aussie sticky-core split sets direction.

symbols.why: Hawkish-re-armed GBP vs a two-sided NZD — mild GBP tilt, low conviction.
symbols.risk: Trade levels only; a hawkish RBNZ closes the gap.

symbols.why: Both commodity-bloc; the live RBA tail nudges a slight AUD tilt but RBNZ Jul 9 keeps it a wash.
symbols.risk: Aussie data vs RBNZ Jul 9.

symbols.why: Firm into Warsh + jobs; PCE + hike-dot validate the leg and the index holds ~101.3, but Sept odds sub-50%; EUR (flash 3.1% softens) / JPY (40-yr low) / GBP / CAD all soft vs USD.
symbols.risk: A cool Jul 2 NFP fades a stretched long; a dovish-surprise Warsh; positioning extreme.

symbols.why: Being run over: basket at fresh lows as the yen breaks 162 to a 40-yr low — core only, reduce/defend, do NOT add. (Rises when JPY strengthens.)
symbols.risk: Wait for a coordinated intervention or a USD pullback to re-load; thesis intact, timing wrong.

symbols.why: Oil tailwind intact (Brent ~$73) + EZ downturn easing + a softer inflation flash; but the Iran re-escalation (weekend Hormuz strikes) widens the tail.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz-talks breakdown / Lebanon escalation gaps it down; a hawkish-Warsh rates scare.

catalysts.event: Warsh — first Sintra appearance (13:00 GMT) + US ISM
catalysts.note: His first international appearance as Chair, on a panel w/ Lagarde, Bailey, Macklem; expected to say little — which is why any hawkish tilt / forward-guidance signal moves markets.
Softer than May; re-softens the EUR and re-firms the EUR/USD short after Monday's Sintra-trim.

catalysts.event: US June jobs report (NFP) — early for Jul 4
catalysts.note: Consensus +130k vs May's hot +172k; a cooler print is the cleanest fade of the firm-but-fading dollar.
Live two-way: Reuters poll + Westpac lean hold, UBS/ING hike — the NZD swing.
Looking through energy CPI; technical recession caps it.
'Gradual' frame (hold 94% priced); June flash 3.1% re-softens the EUR into it.
Warsh's cycle; hot PCE + hike-dot keep it alive — today's panel + Jul 2 jobs the tells.
Services 3.7% keeps 2 hawk dissents live — the GBP swing.
Tokyo core re-accelerating; the yen's structural destination vs the 40-yr-low spot.
USD/JPY broke 162 to ~162.3 (40-yr low); MoF now 'when not if' but doubted — biggest near-term JPY-book swing.
Brent ~$73; weekend US-Iran strikes near Hormuz then a 'stand down' (vessels move freely) — respike tail alive but base case holds.

geopolitics: Flows keep the war premium crushed even as the weekend turned noisy. After Iran struck a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz (Thu), the US ran retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets around the strait Fri-Sat, which Iran called an MOU violation — yet by Monday both sides agreed to 'stand down for now,' confirming vessels can move freely, and Hormuz traffic stayed consistent. Brent ticked up only modestly to ~$73 (still ~40% off the wartime peak); the Doha diplomacy remains muddled (Witkoff/Kushner en route, but Qatar's FM said no high-level meeting is scheduled). Net: the base case (truce broadly holds, oil drifts near multi-month lows) survives, with a live but contained respike tail. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Base case keeps a firm dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD on calm days, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (hot May PCE confirmed inflation marked up into the oil drop; disinflation is energy-led). For NZD the lower oil path removed the cost-shock case the RBNZ leaned on (its 4.3% Q3 peak now looks stale) — the Jul 9 poll now leans hold, though UBS/ING still see a hike, so the kiwi is a genuine coin-flip. For CAD the hot CPI (3.2%, gasoline +33% y/y) only partly offsets the oil drag → soft-and-capped, the export side pressed hardest at ~$73 (loonie near a 14-mo low). Tail (alive but contained): a Doha-talks stall / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,200 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
